note_data.title: Programming scenarios practice
keyPoints: **Programming scenarios** require systematic identification of inputs, processes and outputs before writing any code
**Input identification** involves determining all data that must be entered by the user or read from files
**Process identification** requires breaking down the problem into logical steps including calculations, comparisons and data manipulation
**Output identification** specifies what results must be displayed, printed or stored after processing
**Pseudocode planning** should be completed before writing actual program code to ensure logical flow
**Variable declaration** must include appropriate data types (INTEGER, REAL, STRING, BOOLEAN) based on the data being stored
**Validation checks** should be implemented to ensure input data meets specified criteria before processing
**Testing strategies** must include normal, boundary and erroneous data to verify program functionality
**Code structure** should follow logical sequence: input → validation → process → output with appropriate comments

definitions.definition: A real-world problem that requires a complete program solution including input, processing and output stages
The process of checking that data entered meets specified criteria before it is processed by the program
Test data that uses values at the limits of acceptable ranges to check program handles edge cases correctly
A method of dry-running code by manually tracking variable values through each step of program execution

howToAnswer.instruction: List the specific inputs, processes or outputs without explanation
Produce complete code with correct syntax, variable names and logical structure
Give the method/process and explain how it works using technical terms
Provide specific test data with expected outcomes for normal, boundary and erroneous cases

modelAnswer.question: A school in Lahore needs a program to calculate student exam grades. Students enter their marks for 3 subjects (0-100). The program calculates the average and displays the grade: A (80+), B (70-79), C (60-69), D (50-59), F (below 50). Identify the inputs, processes and outputs for this scenario.
modelAnswer.answer:
INPUTS: Three subject marks (INTEGER values 0-100)
PROCESSES: Validate marks are in range 0-100, Calculate average = (mark1 + mark2 + mark3) / 3, Determine grade using IF statements based on average
OUTPUTS: Average mark (REAL), Grade letter (STRING), Appropriate message displaying results
markBreakdown: Mark 1: Correct identification of three subject marks as inputs

commonMistakes: Students write code immediately without planning inputs, processes and outputs first
Forgetting to include validation checks for input data ranges and data types
Using incorrect data types - mixing up INTEGER and REAL for calculations involving division
Not providing complete test data sets including boundary values and invalid inputs
Writing incomplete IF..THEN..ELSE statements that don't cover all possible grade ranges
examinerTips: Programming scenarios appear in Paper 2 Section B and are worth 15-20 marks
Always plan IPO (Input-Process-Output) before writing any code
Include validation for all inputs to demonstrate good programming practice
Use meaningful variable names that clearly indicate their purpose
Test your logic with boundary values like 79.5 vs 80 for grade boundaries
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: IPO diagram showing three columns: Inputs (user data entry), Processes (calculations and decisions), Outputs (results displayed), with arrows showing data flow from left to right
